body.title: Collective operational learner: escalation corpus + routing compiler
body.result: escalation_corpus.py captures non-DONE signals; routing_compiler.py finds UPGRADE_TIER/SETUP_GAP/REPROMPT_RATE patterns; add_compiled_rule gates auto-apply; 153 tests pass
body.description:
Every escalation is a training signal, not a failure. Right now we discard them after blocking the ticket. The vision: aggregate every non-DONE signal into a corpus with full context, analyze it to find compile-down opportunities, and progressively lower the tier required for each class of work. Over time 'do X' replaces 'CC Opus do X' — the collective routes to whatever capability is sufficient, escalating only as genuinely needed. This is the operational learning loop that makes the collective self-improving, in the same way Igor's Hebbian learning was designed to improve cognition.

Three parts:

**1. Escalation corpus** (flat-file JSONL, no SQLite)
Every non-DONE outcome from inference_dispatch_fn gets appended to ~/.granny/escalation_corpus.jsonl with:
- ticket_id, tags, size, task_class (tier attempted)
- signal (ESCALATE/REPROMPT/UPGRADE/BLOCKED/CONFUSED)
- advisor_signal (post-D2: what the advisor said)
- iterations, cost_usd, tokens_in, tokens_out
- short excerpt of what was attempted (last advisor notes, ≤300 chars)
- timestamp

**2. Routing compiler** (Scraps job, periodic)
Reads corpus, finds patterns:
- 'Platform/S tickets UPGRADE to analyst and succeed → route Platform/S directly to analyst, skip worker attempt'
- 'BLOCKED signals always include "ECONNREFUSED" → this is a setup gap, not a model gap'
- 'REPROMPT rate for Infrastructure tickets > 40% → ticket template for Infrastructure tag needs improvement'
Outputs COMPILE_DOWN_PROPOSALS to channel: proposed changes to rules_engine.py default routing, ranked by confidence and frequency.

**3. Proposal gate**
Proposals are surfaced to CC/Akien for approval before auto-applying. Low-confidence or novel patterns → hold for review. High-confidence patterns (>90%, >20 samples) → auto-apply to rules_engine.py with a commit and channel post.

This is PA2.0 Layer 2→3: discovered relationships become future routing decisions. The graph tree outside Igor learns in exactly the way Igor's memory palace learns — by observation, not by manual curation.

**Affected files:** devices/granny/daemon.py (corpus append hook), new devices/scraps/jobs/routing_compiler.py, devices/inference/rules_engine.py (proposal apply), new tests/scraps/test_routing_compiler.py
**Design rules:** no-sqlite (flat JSONL corpus); log every interface crossing; no-hardcoded-instance-refs; proposals are append-only to the corpus — never mutate historical records
**Scope boundary:** Corpus capture + compiler job + proposal surfacing. Auto-apply only for high-confidence patterns (>90%, >20 samples). Out of scope: learning prompt improvements (that feeds into ticket template work separately); learning from CC sessions (only inference dispatch signals in scope here).
**Gate:** T-tool-loop-advisor (advisor signals are the richest corpus input) should land first, but corpus capture can start immediately with current ESCALATE signals.
**Completion criteria:** ~/.granny/escalation_corpus.jsonl grows with every non-DONE dispatch; routing_compiler.py produces COMPILE_DOWN_PROPOSALS from a seeded test corpus with ≥3 pattern types; `.venv/bin/pytest tests/scraps/ -q -k routing_compiler` passes; at least one auto-apply path tested end-to-end against a mock rules_engine.py
